heading: Competitors
chunk_text: Track competitors in voice dictation, AI writing, and speech productivity. Known competitor categories: - AI dictation apps - Voice-to-text tools - Local transcription tools - Cloud transcription APIs - Productivity voice tools - Developer dictation workflows Competitor names to track manually: - Wispr Flow - Superwhisper - MacWhisper - Aqua Voice - Whisper-based tools - Otter - Descript - Deepgram-based products - Dragon NaturallySpeaking For each competitor, track: - Positioning - Pricing - Distribution channels - Demo style - Main promise - Privacy claims - Onboarding - Viral hooks - Weaknesses - What Vocalype can do better Starter comparison angles: - Vocalype should win on local-first privacy, simple cross-app paste flow, and clear offline defaults. - Competitors may win on polished onboarding, content distribution, or enterprise proof. Track those gaps without copying complexity.

heading: Context Rules
chunk_text: - The model's general knowledge is allowed only as reasoning support. - Facts about Vocalype must come from retrieved memory, repo inspection, metrics, or user-provided data. - If the model has not retrieved evidence, it must say confidence is low. - Do not invent Vocalype architecture details. - Do not recommend unrelated projects. - Always optimize for Vocalype product, distribution, conversion, retention, or revenue. - Every action must include metric, validation test, risk, and next step. - Context window is limited, so retrieve only relevant chunks. - Prefer concise context over huge prompts. - The founder remains final authority. - Autonomous thinking, controlled execution.

heading: Decisions
chunk_text: Record important product, model, pricing, and distribution decisions here. Format: - Date - Decision - Reason - Expected impact - Metric to watch - Review date Useful decisions reduce repeated debate and keep execution focused.

heading: Distribution Log
chunk_text: Track content, launches, outreach, and channel tests. For each item, record: - Date - Channel - Audience - Hook - Demo shown - CTA - Views or impressions - Clicks - Downloads - Lessons Prioritize demos where Vocalype visibly turns speech into usable text inside real apps.

heading: Experiments
chunk_text: Every experiment must include: - Hypothesis - Change - Metric - Start date - End date - Result - Decision Decision options: - keep - kill - iterate Example: Hypothesis: A demo video above the fold will increase download clicks. Change: Add 20-second demo video to homepage hero. Metric: Download button CTR. Decision: Keep if CTR increases by at least 20%.

heading: Founder Rules
chunk_text: The founder's only business focus is Vocalype. The system must protect the founder from distraction. Reject anything that does not improve: - Vocalype product - Vocalype models - Vocalype UX - Vocalype distribution - Vocalype revenue - Vocalype trust - Vocalype retention Do not encourage side businesses. Do not encourage vague AI research unless it directly improves Vocalype. Prefer shipping over perfection. Prefer distribution over endless polishing. Prefer measurable improvements over theory. Every day, produce 5 concrete actions maximum. The founder responds well to direct, honest, high-standard feedback. Tone: - Direct - Strategic - No fake motivation - No vague advice - No overcomplication

heading: Growth Playbook
chunk_text: Vocalype growth should be demo-led. Strong content shows: - Speech turning into text - Text appearing in a real app - A workflow that is faster than typing - A clear privacy or offline advantage - A specific audience pain Prioritize: - Developers dictating into VS Code and ChatGPT - Writers drafting paragraphs without typing - Students capturing notes quickly - Professionals writing emails and documents - People with wrist pain reducing typing strain Avoid: - Generic startup updates - Abstract AI productivity claims - Hooks that hide the product - Content without a demo scene Core metric: View-to-download click rate.

heading: Mistakes
chunk_text: Record decisions, experiments, actions, or content that did not work. For each mistake, capture: - Date - What happened - Why it failed - Metric affected - Lesson - Prevention rule Starter reminders: - Do not chase unrelated AI projects. - Do not polish invisible features before fixing activation. - Do not publish generic startup content when a product demo would be clearer. - Do not change model defaults without benchmarks.

heading: Model Benchmarks
chunk_text: Track speech-to-text model performance with repeatable tests. Model dimensions: - Accuracy - Latency - RAM usage - CPU usage - GPU usage - Startup time - French accuracy - English accuracy - Code dictation accuracy - Punctuation quality - Offline reliability User modes: - Normal user mode - Developer mode - French mode - Low-end PC mode - Privacy mode - Fastest mode - Best accuracy mode Do not recommend model changes without a measurable benchmark.

heading: Model Playbook
chunk_text: Vocalype model decisions must be benchmarked. Do not change defaults based on vibes. Evaluate models by user mode: - Normal user mode: balanced latency and accuracy - Developer mode: code terms, punctuation, identifiers - French mode: French casual and professional speech - Low-end PC mode: RAM, CPU, startup time - Privacy mode: local-only reliability - Fastest mode: lowest usable latency - Best accuracy mode: lowest WER estimate Core benchmark metrics: - median_transcription_latency_ms - wer_estimate - ram_mb - cpu_percent - gpu_percent - startup_time_ms - exact_match_rate for code-sensitive tests Recommendation rule: A model change must improve a target metric without reducing first successful dictation.

heading: Vocalype Product Knowledge
chunk_text: Vocalype is a local-first voice dictation SaaS. Promise: Speak -> text appears -> paste anywhere. Main benefits: - Offline by default - Fast dictation - Private - Simple UX - Works across apps - Useful for writing, coding, emails, notes, ChatGPT prompts, professional documents Core user journey: 1. User lands on website 2. User understands product in less than 5 seconds 3. User downloads app 4. User logs in or activates license 5. User grants permissions 6. User chooses model/settings 7. User starts dictating 8. Text appears 9. User pastes text into another app 10. User understands value and upgrades Most important activation event: First successful dictation. North Star: Number of successful dictations per active user per week. Operational priorities: - Remove anything blocking first successful dictation. - Make privacy and offline behavior obvious before trust is lost

heading: Vocalype Product Knowledge
chunk_text: - Improve model quality with repeatable benchmarks, not guesses. - Convert interested users by showing the product working in real apps. - Prefer small measured improvements over large speculative rewrites.

heading: SaaS Playbook
chunk_text: Vocalype revenue improves when more users understand the promise, complete activation, trust the product, and upgrade. Prioritize funnel work in this order: 1. First successful dictation 2. Download button click rate 3. Permission setup success rate 4. Trial-to-paid conversion 5. Refund reduction 6. Retention and weekly dictation usage Strong SaaS changes: - Clarify offline/private value - Remove activation confusion - Make first dictation fast - Show a real demo before asking for payment - Explain paid value in outcome language Core metrics: - first_dictation_success_rate - download_button_ctr - trial_to_paid_conversion_rate - refund_request_rate

heading: User Feedback
chunk_text: Use this file for qualitative patterns discovered from support, demos, comments, refunds, interviews, and founder observations. Classify feedback by: - activation - onboarding - UX - performance - transcription - pricing - trust - retention Each useful feedback item should become a measurable action or experiment.

heading: Wins
chunk_text: Record actions, experiments, content, and product changes that worked. For each win, capture: - Date - What shipped - Metric improved - Why it worked - How to repeat it Starter patterns to watch: - Short demos that show speech turning into text in real apps - Clear privacy/offline messaging - Reduced steps to first successful dictation - Benchmark-driven model changes
